checklist.title: Set up the AI Dungeon Master
checklist.intro: Four steps take a campaign from no AI to a working AI DM. Each step below reads its real state and links straight to the control that changes it.
checklist.allDoneDriver: The AI holds the DM seat. Players join at the Table tab.
checklist.allDoneCoDm: The AI is co-DMing — it drafts proposals for you to approve. Nothing lands in canon until you say so.

flag.title: Server AI enabled
flag.onAdmin: The server-wide AI switch is on. Any campaign can now use an AI DM.
flag.offAdmin: The server-wide AI switch is off. Turn it on in the AI console to allow any AI participation.
flag.askAdmin: Server-side AI is experimental and off by default. A server admin must enable it before this campaign's AI DM can run.
flag.copyRequest: Please enable experimental AI (Server admin → AI console → Server-wide AI) so I can run an AI DM in my campaign.

provider.title: AI provider configured
provider.done: A provider and API key are stored for this campaign ({{model}}).
provider.todo: Choose a provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, or the mock provider for testing) and store an API key. Test the connection before moving on.

mode.title: Operating mode chosen
mode.doneCoDm: Mode is Co-DM — the AI only proposes; you approve every change.
mode.doneDriver: Mode is Driver — the AI runs the session at the Table.
mode.todo: Pick how the AI participates. Co-DM (recommended) drafts proposals for your review; Driver hands it the DM seat.

budget.title: Token budget set
budget.done: A hard budget of {{budget}} tokens is set. Turns stop when it is reached.
budget.todo: Set a positive token budget. It is a hard cap on spend and is required before Driver mode will run.

table.title: Players know where to play
table.driver: In Driver mode, players join the shared session at the Table tab in the sidebar. Share that with your table.
table.notYet: Once Driver mode is on, a Table tab appears for every member — that's where the session is played.

dashboard.hintBody: This campaign can run an AI co-DM or a fully AI-run Driver session. A few settings turn it on.

badge.coDmAria: An AI is co-DMing this campaign — what that means
badge.driverAria: An AI is running this campaign — open the Table

transparency.title: What the AI sees
transparency.body: The AI DM is given this campaign's summary and its session-zero charter — never any other campaign's data. In Co-DM mode it can only propose changes, which the DM must approve; canon never changes without a human. In Driver mode it runs the session, and the DM (and the table) can pause it or take over at any time from the Table page.

flagDisabled.title: Server AI is turned off
flagDisabled.body: AI features are experimental and disabled server-wide. A server admin must enable them before the AI DM can run.

seatDisabled.title: The AI DM isn't switched on
seatDisabled.body: This campaign's AI DM seat is off. Choose a mode (Co-DM or Driver) and enable it in campaign settings.

budgetExhausted.title: Token budget used up
budgetExhausted.body: The AI DM has spent its token budget. Raise the budget (or reset usage) to continue.

serverCap.title: Server AI budget reached
serverCap.body: The server-wide AI token cap has been reached. A server admin must raise it in the AI console.

needBudget.title: Driver mode needs a budget
needBudget.body: Driver mode requires a positive token budget. Set one, then switch the mode to Driver.

needProvider.title: Driver mode needs a provider
needProvider.body: Driver mode requires a configured AI provider with an API key. Set one, then switch the mode to Driver.

paused.title: The AI DM is paused
paused.body: The session is paused. The DM can resume it from the Table.

humanControl.title: A human holds the seat
humanControl.body: A human has taken over the DM seat. The AI won't act until control is handed back.
